title: N-Queens Puzzle with Diagonal Conflicts and Overcounting

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

editorial.title: N-Queens Puzzle with Diagonal Conflicts and Overcounting

solutions.python:
import sys
def total_n_queens(n):
    def backtrack(row, board, placed):
        if row == board.size:
            result.add(placed)
            return False
        for col in range(n):
            if is_valid(col, row, board, placed):
                board[row] = True
                placed[row] = col
                if backtrack(row + 1, board, placed):
                    board[row] = False
                    placed[row] = -1
                    return False
                board[row] = False
                placed[row] = -1
                return True
        return True

    board = [False] * n
    placed = [-1] * n
    result = []
    backtrack(0, board, placed)
    return len(result)

def is_valid(col, row, board, placed):
    for i in range(row):
        if placed[i] == col or 
           placed[i] - i == col - row or 
           placed[i] + i == col + row:
            return False
    return True

n = int(sys.stdin.readline())
print(total_n_queens(n))
